intent: Reduce overnight power draw during night-lockdown without moving self-hosted services off the desktop, and harden dnsmasq (this PC's LAN DHCP/DNS server) against silently dying again after it was found dead with zero error logged.
scope: Non-goal: migrating services to a Raspberry Pi (tried, abandoned mid-session per user decision after SSH auth failed)
Non-goal: full GPU PCI unbind is built but left disabled by default (GPU_UNBIND_ENABLE=0), not enabled in this change
changes: night-lockdown: new POWER_SAVE_ENABLE (default 1) caps CPU via amd-pstate-epp energy_performance_preference and GPU via nvidia-smi -pl during lockdown, restoring the exact pre-lockdown values at unlock

night-lockdown: prev-value capture guarded on the .prev file not already existing, and unlock deletes it after a successful restore -- closes a crash-safety hole where a kill between capture and LOCKED-state-write would have permanently corrupted the restore baseline on re-invocation
night-lockdown: nvidia-smi -pl calls (both cap and restore) wrapped in timeout 10, matching the existing timeout 20 guard already used for modprobe -r in the same file

night-lockdown: new GPU_UNBIND_ENABLE (default 0, left off) fully unbinds the GPU from the PCI bus as a more aggressive alternative; includes a re-blank_console call after unbind and a bounded driver-ready poll (replacing a fixed sleep) before lightdm restarts, in case it's ever enabled
night-lockdown: extended the existing DRY_RUN NOTE convention to cover the new EPP/nvidia-smi/GPU-unbind steps

setup_dns_blocker.sh: new dnsmasq-watchdog.timer/.service (5-min systemctl is-active check + restart + logger call), mirroring the existing shutdown-timer-monitor-watchdog pattern; enable call centralized in validate_and_enable() (after dnsmasq --test) rather than self-enabling in install_watchdog(), matching this file's own write-then-validate-then-enable convention
phone_focus_mode/config.sh: com.tplink.iot (Tapo) added to both WHITELIST and NIGHT_WHITELIST so the overnight power-draw baseline reading is checkable during the curfew window

verification.evidence: All hooks passed including shellcheck, codespell, secret scan; no lint suppressions added
PASS: test_setup_dns_blocker.sh -- unmodified, covers dispatch + firewall_is_loaded pipefail regression

verification.command: sudo DRY_RUN=1 /usr/local/bin/night-lockdown-enter.sh / -unlock.sh (real deployed scripts, redeployed via setup_night_lockdown.sh setup)
verification.evidence: Correct log output for CPU EPP writes, timeout-wrapped nvidia-smi -pl, new DRY_RUN NOTE lines; state file and no real .prev files touched

verification.command: Live dnsmasq incident (real, not simulated): found dead with zero error logged, mid-session
verification.evidence: Restarted (systemctl restart dnsmasq), confirmed listening on :53/:67, Tapo plug (P110) obtained a DHCP lease at 192.168.1.76 immediately after; watchdog timer deployed and confirmed active via systemctl list-timers

verification.command: 5-agent code review (CLAUDE.md compliance, bug scan, git-history context, code-comment compliance) against the working diff
verification.evidence: 2 bug-scan findings independently verified as false positives on live hardware (nvidia-smi accepts decimal -pl args on this driver version; set -euo pipefail already present, refuting the pipeline-exit-status concern); 3 real findings fixed (install_watchdog enable-ordering, prev-value crash-safety, missing nvidia-smi timeout); 2 gated-off findings (GPU-unbind console-blank race, fixed sleep vs driver-ready poll) fixed proactively since GPU_UNBIND_ENABLE is inert by default

risks: First real (non-DRY_RUN) lockdown cycle with POWER_SAVE_ENABLE=1 has not happened yet -- tonight's scheduled window is the first real-hardware validation

GPU_UNBIND_ENABLE stays default-off; if ever manually enabled, the first real run of that path is also its first real test (self-acknowledged in the config comment, cannot be safely rehearsed since it tears down the same GPU/X session running any interactive test)
Root cause of the dnsmasq death is still unknown (no crash logged) -- the watchdog mitigates recurrence but does not explain the original incident
rollback: Set POWER_SAVE_ENABLE=0 and/or GPU_UNBIND_ENABLE=0 in /etc/night-lockdown.conf, then re-run sudo ./setup_night_lockdown.sh setup to redeploy without the power-save steps
To remove the dnsmasq watchdog: systemctl disable --now dnsmasq-watchdog.timer, rm the two unit files, systemctl daemon-reload
git revert the commit; re-run both setup scripts' setup subcommand to redeploy the prior generated files
After any rollback, verify SSH/WireGuard access before closing the session -- setup_dns_blocker.sh setup reloads the whole nftables ruleset as a side effect
